description.example: Group
A human-readable name for the Group.  REQUIRED.
A list of members of the Group.
Identifier of the member of this Group.
The URI corresponding to a SCIM resource that is a member of this Group.
A label indicating the type of resource, e.g., 'User' or 'Group'.
User Account

Unique identifier for the User, typically used by the user to directly authenticate to the service provider.  Each User MUST include a non-empty userName value.  This identifier MUST be unique across the service provider's entire set of Users.  REQUIRED.

The components of the user's real name.  Providers MAY return just the full name as a single string in the formatted sub-attribute, or they MAY return just the individual component attributes using the other sub-attributes, or they MAY return both.  If both variants are returned, they SHOULD be describing the same name, with the formatted name indicating how the component attributes should be combined.
The full name, including all middle names, titles, and suffixes as appropriate, formatted for display (e.g., 'Ms. Barbara J Jensen, III').
The family name of the User, or last name in most Western languages (e.g., 'Jensen' given the full name 'Ms. Barbara J Jensen, III').
The given name of the User, or first name in most Western languages (e.g., 'Barbara' given the full name 'Ms. Barbara J Jensen, III').
The name of the User, suitable for display to end-users.  The name SHOULD be the full name of the User being described, if known.
The casual way to address the user in real life, e.g., 'Bob' or 'Bobby' instead of 'Robert'.  This attribute SHOULD NOT be used to represent a User's username (e.g., 'bjensen' or 'mpepperidge').
The user's title, such as "Vice President."
A Boolean value indicating the User's administrative status.

Email addresses for the user.  The value SHOULD be canonicalized by the service provider, e.g., 'bjensen@example.com' instead of 'bjensen@EXAMPLE.COM'.  Canonical type values of 'work', 'home', and 'other'.

Email addresses for the user.  The value SHOULD be canonicalized by the service provider, e.g., 'bjensen@example.com' instead of 'bjensen@EXAMPLE.COM'.  Canonical type values of 'work', 'home', and 'other'.
A human-readable name, primarily used for display purposes.  READ-ONLY.
A label indicating the attribute's function, e.g., 'work' or 'home'.
Enterprise User
Numeric or alphanumeric identifier assigned to a person, typically based on order of hire or association with an organization.
Identifies the name of a cost center.
Identifies the name of an organization.
Identifies the name of a department.
The User's manager.  A complex type that optionally allows service providers to represent organizational hierarchy by referencing the 'id' attribute of another User.
The id of the SCIM resource representing the User's manager.  REQUIRED.
The URI of the SCIM resource representing the User's manager.  REQUIRED.
The displayName of the User's manager.  OPTIONAL and READ-ONLY.
Ironclad User
The email address (username) of the user's department head

userName.description: The email address of the user (e.g., jdoe@acme.com).

name.description: The first name and last name of the user.

givenName.description: The first name of the user.

familyName.description: The last name of the user.

emails.description: A list of emails associated with the user. The email with type work and primary flag is typically copied from the main email stored in the userName property.

type.description: The type of email this is. Canonical values include 'work', 'home', and 'other'.

primary.description: Whether this is the user's primary email address.

active.description: Whether this user is currently active. Ironclad users do not have the concept of active vs. inactive, and a SCIM User with active set to `false` will be removed from the company. On subsequent reads, inactive users would be not found.

department.description: Which department the user is a part of.

departmentHead.description: The email address (username) of the user's department head.

allowProviderConversion.description: Whether the user is allowed to convert from `local` to `google` provider.

resourceType.description: Type of the SCIM resource. In this case: 'User'.

lastModified.description: When the User was last modified.

location.description: The URI of the User resource being returned.

externalId.description: An optional identifier of the group sourced from the identity provider.

members.description: The list of users who are members of this group.

type.description: The type of member for this group. This is usually 'User'.

resourceType.description: Type of the SCIM resource. In this case: 'Group'.

location.description: The URI of the Group resource being returned.

XAsUserEmail.name: x-as-user-email
XAsUserEmail.description: Denotes the actor of the request. When used, the API will take into account this user's permissions and access. This or `x-as-user-id` is required when the associated token was produced from the Client Credentials grant or with legacy bearer tokens on select endpoints. More information about [permissions](https://support.ironcladapp.com/s/article/Understanding-and-Managing-Permissions).

XAsUserId.name: x-as-user-id
XAsUserId.description: Denotes the actor of the request. When used, the API will take into account this user's permissions and access. This or `x-as-user-email` is required when the associated token was produced from the Client Credentials grant or with legacy bearer tokens on select endpoints. More information about [permissions](https://support.ironcladapp.com/s/article/Understanding-and-Managing-Permissions).

ExcludedAttributes.name: excludedAttributes
ExcludedAttributes.description: A comma-separated list of attributes to exclude from the resources in the response.

StartIndex.name: startIndex
StartIndex.description: The starting index for retrieving this resource. Resources are indexed based on when they were first added to Ironclad.

Count.name: count
Count.description: Specifies the maximum number of resources to return in a single page of results.

UserId.name: userId
UserId.description: A user's unique id can be identified using the Retrieve all Users endpoint with a filter parameter

GroupId.name: groupId
GroupId.description: A group's unique id can be identified using the Retrieve all Groups endpoint with a filter parameter

SchemaId.name: schemaId
SchemaId.description: A schema's unique id can be identified using the Retrieve all Schemas endpoint

userName.description: The email address of the user (e.g., jdoe@acme.com).

name.description: The first name and last name of the user.

givenName.description: The first name of the user.

familyName.description: The last name of the user.

password.description:
The user's password for username/password type sign in method. Required if there are no SAML configurations set up for the company. 

Password requirements:
- 16+ characters 
- At least one lowercase letter 
- At least one uppercase letter 
- At least one number 
- At least one special character (!"#$%&'()*+,-./:;<=>?@[\]^_`{|}~)

active.description: Whether the user is currently active. Ironclad users cannot be marked as inactive, and will be removed from the company if this is set to false.

department.description: Which department the user is a part of.

departmentHead.description: The email address (username) of the user's department head.

allowProviderConversion.description: Allow the user to convert from 'local' to 'google' provider.

displayName.description: The name you want to give the group in Ironclad.

value.description: The unique id of the user. User Ids can be found by filtering the Retrieve all Users endpoint.

totalResults.description: The total number of users that match this filter. This may be larger than the number of users in this page, in which case pagination may be used to retrieve the next page of results.

Resources.description: The current page of user results.

startIndex.description: The 1-based index of the first result in the current set of list results.

itemsPerPage.description: Specifies the maximum number of resources that can be returned in a single page of results.

status.description: The HTTP status code expressed as a string.
The HTTP status code expressed as a string.

detail.example: No matching schema for attribute: name,lastName

totalResults.description: The total number of groups that match this filter. This may be larger than the number of groups in this page, in which case pagination may be used to retrieve the next page of results.

startIndex.description: The 1-based index of the first result in the current set of list results.

itemsPerPage.description: Specifies the maximum number of resources that can be returned in a single page of results.

status.description: The HTTP status code expressed as a string.
The HTTP status code expressed as a string.

detail.example: No matching schema for attribute: legalName

get.description:
Documentation on listing Users via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.users.readUsers`

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filters for identifying subsets of users. Filters must comply with the SCIM protocol's convention. For more details, see SCIM [RFC 7644 Section 3.4.2.2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7644#section-3.4.2.2).

post.description:
Documentation on creating a User via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.users.createUsers`

get.description:
Documentation on accessing User info via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.users.readUsers`

patch.description:
Documentation on updating a User via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.users.updateUsers`

path.description: The path of the attribute that you wish to change (e.g., emails, name.givenName, name.familyName)

value.description: The value you wish to use for "add" or "replace" operations. The "remove" operation does not take the "value" parameter.

put.description:
Documentation on replacing a User via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.users.updateUsers`

delete.description:
Delete a user on Ironclad. If the deletion is successful, all active workflows associated with the deleted user should be automatically reassigned to the default user within the admin group. To learn how to set a default user, see the [Manage Groups](https://support.ironcladapp.com/hc/en-us/articles/12286570480791-Manage-Groups) guide. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.users.deleteUsers`

get.description:
Documentation on listing Groups via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.groups.readGroups`

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filters for identifying subsets of groups. Filters must comply with the SCIM protocol's convention. For more details, see SCIM [RFC 7644 Section 3.4.2.2](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7644#section-3.4.2.2).

post.description:
Documentation on creating a Group via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.groups.createGroups`

get.description:
Documentation on accessing a Group via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.groups.readGroups`

patch.description:
Documentation on updating a Group via SCIM. This includes updating group membership. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.groups.updateGroups`

Operations.description: Operations for updating groups. See snippets below for more details.

value.description: An array of User Ids. User Ids can be found by filtering results from the Retrieve all Users endpoint.
The unique id of the user. User Ids can be found by filtering the Retrieve all Users endpoint.

put.description:
Documentation on replacing a Group via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.groups.updateGroups`

delete.description:
Documentation on deleting a Group via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.groups.deleteGroups`

get.description:
Documentation on listing all Schemas via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.schemas.readSchemas`

Documentation on retrieving a schema via SCIM. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `scim.schemas.readSchemas`
